title: calophyllum costatum
intro: Calophyllum costatum, also known as Calophyllum soulattri, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its large, elliptical leaves and vibrant yellow flowers. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its use in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Calophyllum costatum include its potential to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
